sourceIntent: Introduce /openflow-implement as OpenFlow's explicit implementation-stage entrypoint that starts the available execution backend without duplicating writing-plan, Prometheus, omo /start-work, or openflow-quality-gate responsibilities.
assumptions: Plan quality belongs to openflow-writing-plan / Prometheus, not /openflow-implement.
omo /start-work is wrapped and started when omo is installed.
OpenFlow must support users who do not install omo by starting OpenCode native build execution.
constraints: Do not duplicate plan preflight in /openflow-implement.
Do not require users to manually pass worktree paths to quality-gate.
Do not make omo installation a hard requirement for the implementation phase.
Do not determine implementation status solely from chat history.

inScope: Add an implementation-stage workflow boundary for OpenFlow.
Record implementation context including worktree and execution engine.
Support explicit omo and OpenCode native execution backend states.
Record worktree lifecycle and implementation observations.
Commit derived worktree implementations during archive.
Prepare future Project Orchestrator node protocol.

acceptanceCriteria.description: /openflow-implement marks a feature as implementation active and records implementation context.
Implementation context can include a feature-specific worktree without exposing it as a required quality-gate argument.
Users without omo execute through OpenCode native build rather than receiving a broken /start-work instruction.
Implementation execution produces machine-readable events and human-readable observations.
Worktree lifecycle records path, branch, base ref, owner, and cleanup policy.
Derived worktree implementations are committed during archive and the commit hash is recorded.

goals: Create a first-class OpenFlow implementation phase boundary.
Preserve existing writing-plan, start-work, and quality-gate responsibilities.
Keep omo optional while still offering a first-class implementation phase that starts execution.
Make implementation execution observable through state, events, and observations.
Bind derived worktree commits to archive rather than implementation or quality-gate.
nonGoals: Force users to manage worktree paths manually.
Require omo installation for all OpenFlow implementation workflows.
Infer final readiness from chat completion phrases alone.
Commit derived worktree changes before archive artifacts are produced.
problemStatement: OpenFlow lacks a first-class implementation-stage entrypoint and persisted implementation context, especially for worktree-isolated execution.
